_comment: TEMPLATE — copy to evidata_config.json and fill in. This .example file is safe to commit. The real evidata_config.json should NOT be committed (add it to .gitignore) since it carries environment-specific endpoint values. No secrets live in either file — only env-var NAMES; the secret values stay in the separate env_file (see below).
_comment_enabled: Master switch. false (or this whole file absent) => preflight skipped silently, pipeline behaves as if EviData never existed. true => preflight runs PER FILE before clinical ingestion: each CSV is checked individually, files that pass are ingested, files that fail/error are SKIPPED (not ingested) and retried next run. The whole project is NOT aborted on a single failure. The flag is GLOBAL — there is no per-project enable/disable.
_comment_endpoints: Both URLs point at the EviData deployment. If EviData is fronted by a TLS reverse proxy (recommended), use https:// with no port — the proxy routes /api to the EviData API and /auth to Keycloak. If you must hit the containers directly, the API is on :8000 and Keycloak on :3000. Replace <evidata-host> and <keycloak-realm> accordingly.
_comment_client_id: Keycloak PROGRAMMATIC client id (not the browser/UI client). 'evidata-api' is the install-doc default; confirm with whoever deployed the stack.
_comment_scope: OAuth2 scope requested in the token grant. MUST include 'openid' — the EviData API rejects bearer tokens issued without it (HTTP 401 'Failed to authenticate user') even though Keycloak returns the token with 200. Optional key; EviDataClient defaults to this same value if omitted.
_comment_secrets: These are env-var NAMES, not values. EviDataClient::authenticate() reads the actual values via getenv() and throws if any of the three is empty. Set them in the env_file below (auto-loaded by the pipeline runner) or export them in the shell.
_comment_env_file: Path to the file holding the three credential env vars (EVIDATA_CLIENT_SECRET / EVIDATA_USERNAME / EVIDATA_PASSWORD as `export KEY=value` lines). run_clinical_pipeline.php and test_evidata_connection.php auto-load it, so a forgotten `source` does not break a run. The file MUST be chmod 600 and gitignored — it holds secrets. Resolution order: EVIDATA_ENV_FILE env var > this key > hardcoded default. A real environment variable, if already set, always wins over the file.
_comment_polling: Async report generation tuning. poll_interval_seconds = gap between status checks; poll_timeout_seconds = give-up deadline. Both optional (EviDataClient defaults: 10 / 600).
_comment_report_metadata: Static labels stamped on every report. analysis_type is also sent with the dataset upload. All optional (EviDataClient supplies defaults).
_comment_qis: GLOBAL BASELINE quasi-identifier list. QI lists are resolved at TWO levels: (1) project.json -> evidata.qis is the per-project override — when a project defines a non-empty qis, that project fully owns its QI policy; (2) this global qis is the fallback, used ONLY by projects that do NOT define their own. Keep this global value a simple FLAT ARRAY — a per-filename map here would wrongly apply the same filename rule to every project that falls back to it (many projects share filenames like redcap_data.csv). Per-project / per-file granularity belongs in each project's project.json. EviData's server-side QI validation is CASE-SENSITIVE: every QI name must match the CSV header casing exactly.
_comment_qis_all_headers_default: IMPORTANT — ALL-HEADERS DEFAULT: if qis is EMPTY ([]) or ABSENT at BOTH levels (no project.json evidata.qis and this global qis empty/absent), the pipeline uses EVERY column header in each CSV as a QI, automatically per file. Two automatic reductions apply in this mode: (a) columns listed in exclude_qis are dropped; (b) columns whose every value is unique-per-row (identifiers / pseudonyms / timestamps such as StudyID) are dropped automatically, because EviData treats them as QIs and they force maximum re-identification risk. Per-file log records which columns were used and which were excluded. Set qis to [] (empty) here to deliberately select the all-headers default globally.
_comment_exclude_qis: Optional. Column names to DROP when running in all-headers mode (ignored when an explicit qis list is in effect). Two-level like qis: project.json -> evidata.exclude_qis overrides this global list. Matching is CASE-INSENSITIVE (exclusion is deliberately liberal). Use it to remove known non-identifying or identifier columns from the all-headers set, on top of the automatic unique-per-row removal.
_comment_population_size: Reference population size. Must be >= (CSV rows + 1). Optional (default 50000).
_comment_from_address: Optional. From: address on the privacy-failure email. Omit to fall back to archimedes-pipeline@<hostname>.
